# gRPC Connector - Full Documentation

# Using Anypoint Code Builder to Configure gRPC Connector 1.0

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

**Open Beta Release**: The **cloud IDE is in open beta**. Any use of Anypoint Code Builder in its
beta state is subject to the applicable beta services terms and conditions, available from the IDE.

To use Anypoint Code Builder to configure a connector or module, create a basic integration project
in Anypoint Code Builder, add connectors and other components to your Mule application to process
your business logic, and configure the attributes.

## Before You Begin

Before creating an integration project, you must:

- [Set up and access the Anypoint Code Builder web or desktop IDE](../../anypoint-code-builder/start-acb)
- Have credentials to access the connectorâs API
- Ensure that the Visual Studio Code Extension Pack for Java is installed.

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The connectorâs API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)

## Create a New Integration Project

To create a new integration project:

1.  In the activity bar of the IDE, click the
    ![Anypoint Code Builder icon](../../connectors/_images/acb-icon-ms-logo.png) (**Anypoint Code
    Builder**) icon.
2.  From **Quick Actions**, click **Develop an Integration**:

    ![Develop an Integration link highlighted in the Getting started section](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/develop-integration-option-mat.png)

3.  Complete the following in the **Develop an Integration** form:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Project Name</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unique name for your project.</p></div><div><p>This name is used as the title and name of the integration project file. For example, if the project name is "Salesforce Integration," the project file name is <code>salesforce-integration</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Project Location</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Your home directory or another directory you create. Click <strong>Browse</strong> to select a different directory for the integration project.</p></div><div><p>Donât create the project within another project directory.</p></div><div><p>See <a href="../../anypoint-code-builder/start-add-folders">Adding Folders to Your Home Directory</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

4.  Select the type of project to create by selecting either the **Empty Project** or **Template or
    Example** card.
5.  Select the **Mule runtime** and **Java Version** for your app.
6.  Click **Create Project**.

## Add the Connector to Your Integration Project

Anypoint Connectors provide operations for retrieving, modifying, and sending data to and from
systems.

In addition to the
[built-in connectors](../../anypoint-code-builder/acb-reference#builtin-connectors) that Anypoint
Code Builder provides, you can download many other connectors from Anypoint Exchange.

To import a connector from Exchange and add it to your configuration:

1.  In the Explorer view, open the configuration XML file for your project, such as
    `my-project-name.xml`.
2.  Click the
    ![Show Mule graphical mode icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-tree-view.png) (**Show
    Mule graphical mode**) icon in the activity bar to open the canvas UI if it doesnât open
    automatically.
3.  Add the connector the same way you added other components from the canvas UI:
    1.  In the canvas UI, click the
        ![Add component icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-plus.png) (**Add component**)
        icon.
    2.  In the **Add Component** panel, click **Connectors**.
    3.  Click the connector name and then click the operation to add, such as **Publish**:

        ![Add Publish operation from Anypoint MQ Connector](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/int-add-connector-operation.png)

        If the connector is not available locally, click the
        ![Search in Exchange icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-search-exchange.png)
        (**Search in Exchange**) toggle:

        ![Search in Exchange toggle](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/int-connector-search-exchange.png)

        <table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Search locally</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td>Search in Exchange</td></tr></tbody></table>

    4.  Select the connector to add to your project.
    5.  Select the operation from the **Add Component** panel.

Adding a connector to an integration project does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Anypoint Code Builder workspace.

## Configure the Source For the Flow

A trigger (source) starts the flow when specified conditions are met. If a connector has its own
associated sources, you can use a connector-specific trigger to initiate the flow. Each connector
trigger has a unique configuration.

You can configure the following sources for this connector:

- **HTTP > Listener**

  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port

- **Scheduler**

  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure an HTTP Listener trigger, follow these steps:

1.  Click the **Listener** component on the canvas UI.
2.  (Optional) Click the edit icon to change the name of the **Listener** component.
3.  (Optional) If you created a configuration file for the connector, select it.
4.  In the **General** tab, in the **Path** field, enter the relative path for the path set in the
    HTTP configuration element. See
    [Configure Paths](../../http-connector/latest/http-listener-ref#configure-paths).
5.  Select the **Advanced** tab to configure the following attributes:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Allowed methods</p></td><td><p>To allow all HTTP methods (default), do not define this attribute. To restrict certain methods, specify all allowed HTTP methods, for example, <code>GET, POST</code>, as a comma-separated list.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response streaming mode</p></td><td><p>Whether to use streaming when the response is sent.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output MIME type</p></td><td><p>MIME type of the payload that this component outputs.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Parameters</p></td><td><p>Appends a key-value pair for a reader property to the value of the Output MIME attribute. Multiple key-value pairs are allowed.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output encoding</p></td><td><p>Encoding of the payload that this component outputs.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><p>Configures the way data streams are consumed in Mule apps.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary node only</p></td><td><p>Select this option to execute the <strong>Listener</strong> trigger on the primary node only when running in a cluster.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery policy</p></td><td><p>Configures the redelivery policy for executing requests that generate errors. You can add a redelivery policy to any source in a flow.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection strategy</p></td><td><p>When an operation in a Mule application fails to connect to an external server, the default behavior is for the operation to fail immediately and return a connectivity error. You can modify the default behavior by configuring the reconnection strategy for the operation.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response</p></td><td><p>See <a href="../../http-connector/latest/http-documentation#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Response</p></td><td><p>HTTP error response returns a status code for the error.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><p>Body of the error message.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Headers</p></td><td><p>Headers to include in the error message.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Status code</p></td><td><p>Status code of the error response, for example, <code>500</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reason phrase</p></td><td><p>Reason for the error, for example, <code>Gateway Timeout</code> or <code>Internal Server Error</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Add Components to Your Project

Add components to your integration project to build a flow:

1.  In the **Explorer** view, open the XML file for your project, such as `my-project-name.xml`.
2.  Select **Build a Flow** from the start card to create an empty flow:

    ![Anypoint Code Builder canvas with starting cards with the options to start from scratch, build a flow, or build a subflow](../../connectors/_images/acb-build-flow.png)

3.  Change the name of the flow:
    1.  Click **Flow**.
    2.  Click the edit icon.

        ![Arrow pointing to the flow name edit pencil icon](../../connectors/_images/acb-change-flow-name.png)

    3.  Enter the flow name, and click the checkmark.
    4.  In the canvas UI, click the
        ![Add component icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-plus.png) (**Add component**)
        icon.
    5.  In the **Add Component** panel, search for and select your component from the results. The
        following example shows the **Listener** component from the **HTTP** search results:

        ![Listener component highlighted in the Add Component section](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/main-tutorial-add-first-listener.png)

        The configuration XML file now includes the XML for the HTTP Listener into the `<flow/>`
        element, for example:

        ```XML
        <flow name="getFlights" >
          <http:listener path="" config-ref="" doc:name="Listener" doc:id="ojzfry" />
        </flow>
        ```

## Configure the Component Attributes

After you add components to your integration project, configure the attributes in the component UI,
or configure the attributes directly in the XML code editor.

Connector XML tags start with a namespace followed by an operation, following the syntax
`namespace:operation`, such as `<db:select/>` for a **Select** operation in Anypoint Connector for
Database (Database Connector).

You can use autocomplete from an expression (**fx**) field for a component to select DataWeave
functions from the Core module and other values accepted in this field.

### Client Configuration

```xml
<grpc:client-config name="GRPC_Client_Config">
    <grpc:connection host="localhost" port="50051">
        <grpc:idl-definition>
            <grpc:single-binary-protobuf protobufDescriptorFile="path/to/service.desc" />
        </grpc:idl-definition>
    </grpc:connection>
</grpc:client-config>
```

### Server Configuration

```xml
<grpc:config name="GRPC_Server_Config">
    <grpc:connection>
        <grpc:grpc-server listenerConfig="HTTP_Listener_config">
            <grpc:idl-definition>
                <grpc:single-binary-protobuf protobufDescriptorFile="path/to/service.desc" />
            </grpc:idl-definition>
        </grpc:grpc-server>
    </grpc:connection>
</grpc:config>
```

### Client Operations

#### \[Client\] Bidirectional Stream Message

```xml
<grpc:bidirectional-streaming
    config-ref="GRPC_Client_Config"
    method="ServiceName/BidiStreamingMethod"
    routingId="#[vars.routingId]"
    requestId="#[vars.requestId]">
    <grpc:content>#[payload]</grpc:content>
</grpc:bidirectional-streaming>
```

#### \[Client\] Client Streaming Call

```xml
<grpc:client-streaming
    config-ref="GRPC_Client_Config"
    method="ServiceName/ClientStreamingMethod"
    requestId="#[vars.requestId]">
    <grpc:content>#[payload]</grpc:content>
</grpc:client-streaming>
```

#### \[Client\] Server Stream Message

```xml
<grpc:server-streaming-call-with-delegation
    config-ref="GRPC_Client_Config"
    method="ServiceName/StreamingMethod"
    routingId="#[vars.routingId]">
    <grpc:content>
        #[payload]
    </grpc:content>
</grpc:server-streaming-call-with-delegation>
```

#### \[Client\] Iterate Server Stream Message

```xml
<grpc:server-streaming-call-with-response
    config-ref="GRPC_Client_Config"
    method="ServiceName/StreamingMethod">
    <grpc:content>
        #[payload]
    </grpc:content>
</grpc:server-streaming-call-with-response>
```

#### \[Client\] Unary Message

```xml
<grpc:unary-call config-ref="GRPC_Client_Config" method="ServiceName/MethodName">
    <grpc:content>
        #[%dw 2.0
        output application/x-protobuf messageType='package.MessageType', descriptorUrl="path/to/service.desc"
        ---
        {
            field1: "value1",
            field2: 123
        }]
    </grpc:content>
</grpc:unary-call>
```

### Client Sources

#### \[Client\] Bidirectional Stream Listener

```xml
<grpc:bidi-streaming-listener
    config-ref="GRPC_Client_Config"
    routingId="#[vars.routingId]"/>
```

#### \[Client\] Server Stream Listener

```xml
<grpc:server-streaming-delegated-listener
    config-ref="GRPC_Client_Config"
    routingId="#[vars.routingId]"/>
```

#### \[Client\] Server Stream Subscriber

```xml
<grpc:server-streaming-subscriber-source
    config-ref="GRPC_Client_Config"
    method="ServiceName/StreamingMethod"
    filePath="path/to/request.bin"/>
```

### Server Operations

#### \[Server\] End Stream

```xml
<grpc:end-stream config-ref="GRPC_Server_Config" attributes="#[attributes]"/>
```

#### \[Server\] Respond Server Stream Message

```xml
<grpc:send-stream-message config-ref="GRPC_Server_Config" attributes="#[attributes]" streamMessage="#[payload]"/>
```

### Server Sources

#### \[Server\] Bidirectional Streaming Listener

```xml
<grpc:bidirectional-streaming-method config-ref="GRPC_Server_Config" methodName="ServiceName/BidiStreamingMethod">
    <grpc:response-bidi streamStates="#[vars.updatedStreamStates]"/>
</grpc:bidirectional-streaming-method>
```

#### \[Server\] Client Stream Listener

```xml
<grpc:client-streaming-method config-ref="GRPC_Server_Config" methodName="ServiceName/ClientStreamingMethod"/>
```

#### \[Server\] Server Stream Listener

```xml
<flow name="ServerStreamingFlow">
  <grpc:server-streaming-method config-ref="GRPC_Server_Config" methodName="ServiceName/StreamingMethod"/>
  <grpc:send-stream-message config-ref="GRPC_Server_Config" attributes="#[attributes]" streamMessage="#[payload]"/>
  <grpc:end-stream config-ref="GRPC_Server_Config" attributes="#[attributes]"/>
</flow>
```

#### \[Server\] Unary Listener

```xml
<grpc:unary-method config-ref="GRPC_Server_Config" methodName="ServiceName/MethodName" responseUnary="#[payload]"/>
```

## Create a New Configuration XML File

Anypoint Code Builder creates the initial Mule configuration XML file in `src/main/mule` within the
Mule project. This file contains global configurations and flow structures.

You can create additional configuration files, for example, to store global configurations.

To create a new XML configuration file:

1.  In the Explorer view, right-click the **mule** folder (`src/main/mule`) and select **New File**.

    Alternatively, use the menu.

    Show me how

    Select the `mule` folder, and then:
    - In the desktop IDE, select **File** > **New Mule Configuration File**.
    - In the cloud IDE, click the ![](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-menu.png) (menu)
      icon, and select **File** > **New File**.

2.  Enter a name for the new file with the `.xml` extension.
3.  In the Editor view for the new file, press Ctrl+Space to display available options.
4.  Select **Empty mule configuration**:

    ![New Mule configuration option in auto-correct](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/int-new-mule-config.png)

    This command adds the following code to the file:

    ```xml
    <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
    <mule xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
        xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
        xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
        xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
        http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd">

    </mule>
    ```

# gRPC Connector 1.0 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for gRPC (gRPC Connector) enables you to connect to the gRPC API.

## Configurations

---

### Client

Client configuration for connecting to gRPC services.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Client_GoogleServiceAccountAuthProvider">Google Service Account Auth Provider</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Client_NoAuth">No Auth</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Client_OauthClientCredentials">Oauth Client Credentials</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Deadline</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Deadline">Deadline</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Deadline configuration for gRPC calls. This deadline applies to all operations unless overridden at the operation level.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Wait Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Time to wait for server response after sending each message in client streaming. Overridable at the operation level. A value of 0 means no wait (return immediately with an empty continuation response).</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Wait Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Wait Time</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>MILLISECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The identifier of this element used to reference it in other components.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This doesnât mean that the platform expires the instance at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Google Service Account Auth Provider

Use when the gRPC server expects Google-style service account auth (such as Google Cloud).

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The host address of the gRPC server.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The port number of the gRPC server.</p></td><td><p>443</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>IDL Definition</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#SingleBinaryProtobuf">Single Binary Protobuf</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The Interface Definition Language (IDL) definition for the gRPC service.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Request headers to include in gRPC requests.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for establishing connections to the remote service.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Mode</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>PlainText</p></li><li><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Connection mode: TLS (default, for production) or PlainText (for development). If not specified, TLS with default settings is used automatically.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Private key id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Identifier of the private key for the service account. Part of service credentials.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Identifier of the client. Part of service credentials.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client email</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Client email for the service account. Part of service credentials.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Private key</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#PrivateKeyText">Private Key Text</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#PrivateKeyFile">Private Key File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Private key for the service account. Part of service credentials. Can be provided as text or from a file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### No Auth

Use when the gRPC server does not require authentication (such as local or internal services).

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The host address of the gRPC server.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The port number of the gRPC server.</p></td><td><p>443</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>IDL Definition</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#SingleBinaryProtobuf">Single Binary Protobuf</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The Interface Definition Language (IDL) definition for the gRPC service.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in gRPC requests.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for establishing connections to the remote service.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Mode</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>PlainText</p></li><li><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Connection mode: TLS (default, for production) or PlainText (for development). If not specified, TLS with default settings is used automatically.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Oauth Client Credentials

Use when the gRPC server expects OAuth 2.0 client credentials. The connector obtains a token and
sends it in the authorization header.

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The host address of the gRPC server.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The port number of the gRPC server.</p></td><td><p>443</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>IDL Definition</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#SingleBinaryProtobuf">Single Binary Protobuf</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The Interface Definition Language (IDL) definition for the gRPC service.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in gRPC requests.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for establishing connections to the remote service.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Mode</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>PlainText</p></li><li><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Connection mode: TLS (default, for production) or PlainText (for development). If not specified, TLS with default settings is used automatically.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorization Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The header key used for authorization.</p></td><td><p>Authorization</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth client ID as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth client secret as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Token Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service providerâs token endpoint URL.</p></td><td><p><code>https://example.com/oauth2/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth scopes to be requested during the dance. If not provided, it defaults to those in the annotation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Auth Server Tls Context</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>References a TLS config to use to do HTTP request during the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Auth Server Proxy Config</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>References the proxy configuration which through the HTTP request during the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A reference to the object store that should be used to store each resource owner idâs data. If not specified, runtime will automatically provision the default one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operations

- [\[Client\] Bidirectional Stream Message](#BidirectionalStreaming)
- [\[Client\] Client Streaming Call](#ClientStreaming)
- [\[Client\] Server Stream Message](#ServerStreamingCallWithDelegation)
- [\[Client\] Iterate Server Stream Message](#ServerStreamingCallWithResponse)
- [\[Client\] Unary Message](#UnaryCall)
- [Unauthorize](#Unauthorize)

## Associated Sources

- [\[Client\] Bidirectional Stream Listener](#BidiStreamingListener)
- [\[Client\] Server Stream Listener](#ServerStreamingDelegatedListener)
- [\[Client\] Server Stream Subscriber](#ServerStreamingSubscriberSource)

---

### GRPC Server Configuration

Server configuration for exposing gRPC methods.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Server_GrpcServer">Grpc Server</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>The connection to provide to this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Inbound Message Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum size of an inbound message in bytes (default: 4MB, same as gRPC-Java). Must be a positive value.</p></td><td><p>4194304</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The identifier of this element used to reference it in other components.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Grpc Server

Reference an `<http:listener-config>` for the gRPC endpoint and provide the protobuf descriptor for
your service.

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Listener Config</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Reference to the <code>&lt;http:listener-config&gt;</code> used to expose the gRPC server endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>IDL Definition</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#SingleBinaryProtobuf">Single Binary Protobuf</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Protobuf descriptor definition for the gRPC service.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operations

- [\[Server\] End Stream](#EndStream)
- [\[Server\] Respond Server Stream Message](#SendStreamMessage)

## Associated Sources

- [\[Server\] Bidirectional Streaming Listener](#BidirectionalStreamingMethod)
- [\[Server\] Client Stream Listener](#ClientStreamingMethod)
- [\[Server\] Server Stream Listener](#ServerStreamingMethod)
- [\[Server\] Unary Listener](#UnaryMethod)

## Operations

## \[Client\] Bidirectional Stream Message

`<grpc:bidirectional-streaming>`

Connect to a Bidirectional Streaming RPC. This operation initiates a request, and its responses are
collected by the corresponding [\[Client\] Bidirectional Stream Listener](#BidiStreamingListener).
[\[Client\] Bidirectional Stream Message](#BidirectionalStreaming) and
[\[Client\] Bidirectional Stream Listener](#BidiStreamingListener) are mapped to each other via
**Routing ID**. This operation can be used to initiate the client up-stream, send requests, and also
close the client up-stream. Each of these client steps can be mapped using a common request ID.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Method</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The gRPC method name to invoke.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Routing Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Identifier to map this call with its corresponding listener.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Identifier to map related client streaming steps.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>The request payload to send to the gRPC server.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>End Streaming</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether to close the client streaming.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the gRPC request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Deadline</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Deadline">Deadline</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the deadline for this specific gRPC call. Overrides the configuration-level deadline if specified.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Client](#Client)

### Throws

- GRPC:ABORTED
- GRPC:ALREADY_EXISTS
- GRPC:CANCELLED
- GRPC:CONNECTIVITY
- GRPC:DATA_LOSS
- GRPC:DEADLINE_EXCEEDED
- GRPC:FAILED_PRECONDITION
- GRPC:GRPC_UNKNOWN
- GRPC:INTERNAL
- GRPC:INVALID_ARGUMENT
- GRPC:NOT_FOUND
- GRPC:OUT_OF_RANGE
- GRPC:PERMISSION_DENIED
- GRPC:RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED
- GRPC:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GRPC:TRANSFORMATION
- GRPC:UNAUTHENTICATED
- GRPC:UNAVAILABLE
- GRPC:UNIMPLEMENTED
- GRPC:VALIDATION

## \[Client\] Client Streaming Call

`<grpc:client-streaming>`

Connect to a Client Streaming RPC. This single operation can be used to initiate streaming, send
requests, and close the client streaming request. Each of these steps is correlated via a common
request ID.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Method</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The gRPC method name to invoke.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Identifier to map related client streaming steps.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>The request payload to send to the gRPC server.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>End Streaming</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether to close the client streaming.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the gRPC request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Deadline</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Deadline">Deadline</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the deadline for this specific gRPC call. Overrides the configuration-level deadline if specified.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Wait Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Time to wait for server response after sending each message. Overrides the configuration-level value if specified.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Wait Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Wait Time</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ClientStreamingAttributes">Client Streaming Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Client](#Client)

### Throws

- GRPC:ABORTED
- GRPC:ALREADY_EXISTS
- GRPC:CANCELLED
- GRPC:CONNECTIVITY
- GRPC:DATA_LOSS
- GRPC:DEADLINE_EXCEEDED
- GRPC:FAILED_PRECONDITION
- GRPC:GRPC_UNKNOWN
- GRPC:INTERNAL
- GRPC:INVALID_ARGUMENT
- GRPC:NOT_FOUND
- GRPC:OUT_OF_RANGE
- GRPC:PERMISSION_DENIED
- GRPC:RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED
- GRPC:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GRPC:TRANSFORMATION
- GRPC:UNAUTHENTICATED
- GRPC:UNAVAILABLE
- GRPC:UNIMPLEMENTED
- GRPC:VALIDATION

## \[Client\] Server Stream Message

`<grpc:server-streaming-call-with-delegation>`

Connect to a Server Streaming RPC. This operation initiates a request, and its responses are
collected by the corresponding Server Streaming Delegated Listener. The Server Streaming Delegator
and Server Streaming Delegated Listener are mapped to each other via the routing ID.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Method</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The gRPC method name to invoke.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Routing Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Identifier to map this call with its corresponding listener.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>The request payload to send to the gRPC server.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the gRPC request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Deadline</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Deadline">Deadline</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the deadline for this specific gRPC call. Overrides the configuration-level deadline if specified.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Client](#Client)

### Throws

- GRPC:ABORTED
- GRPC:ALREADY_EXISTS
- GRPC:CANCELLED
- GRPC:CONNECTIVITY
- GRPC:DATA_LOSS
- GRPC:DEADLINE_EXCEEDED
- GRPC:FAILED_PRECONDITION
- GRPC:GRPC_UNKNOWN
- GRPC:INTERNAL
- GRPC:INVALID_ARGUMENT
- GRPC:NOT_FOUND
- GRPC:OUT_OF_RANGE
- GRPC:PERMISSION_DENIED
- GRPC:RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED
- GRPC:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GRPC:TRANSFORMATION
- GRPC:UNAUTHENTICATED
- GRPC:UNAVAILABLE
- GRPC:UNIMPLEMENTED
- GRPC:VALIDATION

## \[Client\] Iterate Server Stream Message

`<grpc:server-streaming-call-with-response>`

Connect to a Server Streaming RPC. This operation returns a StreamedResponseIterable to iterate over
server response-stream.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Method</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The gRPC method name to invoke.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>The request payload to send to the gRPC server.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the gRPC request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Deadline</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Deadline">Deadline</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the deadline for this specific gRPC call. Overrides the configuration-level deadline if specified.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Binary</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Client](#Client)

### Throws

- GRPC:ABORTED
- GRPC:ALREADY_EXISTS
- GRPC:CANCELLED
- GRPC:CONNECTIVITY
- GRPC:DATA_LOSS
- GRPC:DEADLINE_EXCEEDED
- GRPC:FAILED_PRECONDITION
- GRPC:GRPC_UNKNOWN
- GRPC:INTERNAL
- GRPC:INVALID_ARGUMENT
- GRPC:NOT_FOUND
- GRPC:OUT_OF_RANGE
- GRPC:PERMISSION_DENIED
- GRPC:RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED
- GRPC:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GRPC:TRANSFORMATION
- GRPC:UNAUTHENTICATED
- GRPC:UNAVAILABLE
- GRPC:UNIMPLEMENTED
- GRPC:VALIDATION

## \[Client\] Unary Message

`<grpc:unary-call>`

Invokes a standard gRPC method that accepts a single request and returns a single response. Use this
operation to perform an individual, one-off gRPC call to a remote service method.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Method</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A String object.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>An InputStream object.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the gRPC request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Deadline</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Deadline">Deadline</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the deadline for this specific gRPC call. Overrides the configuration-level deadline if specified.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Client](#Client)

### Throws

- GRPC:ALREADY_EXISTS
- GRPC:CONNECTIVITY
- GRPC:DATA_LOSS
- GRPC:DEADLINE_EXCEEDED
- GRPC:FAILED_PRECONDITION
- GRPC:GRPC_UNKNOWN
- GRPC:INTERNAL
- GRPC:INVALID_ARGUMENT
- GRPC:NOT_FOUND
- GRPC:OUT_OF_RANGE

- GRPC:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

- GRPC:VALIDATION

## Unauthorize

`<grpc:unauthorize>`

Deletes all the access token information of a given resource owner ID so that itâs impossible to
execute any operation for that user without doing the authorization dance again.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Client](#Client)

## \[Server\] End Stream

`<grpc:end-stream>`

Ends the streaming response.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ServerStreamingServerAttributes">Server Streaming Server Attributes</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Streaming attributes from the server streaming source.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## \[Server\] Respond Server Stream Message

`<grpc:send-stream-message>`

Sends a message in a streaming response.

- [GRPC Server Configuration](#Server)

## Sources

## \[Client\] Bidirectional Stream Listener

`<grpc:bidi-streaming-listener>`

Connects to a Bidirectional Streaming RPC. Server responses from the corresponding
[\[Client\] Bidirectional Stream Message](#BidirectionalStreaming) are collected here.
[\[Client\] Bidirectional Stream Message](#BidirectionalStreaming) and
[\[Client\] Bidirectional Stream Listener](#BidiStreamingListener) are mapped to each other via
**Routing ID**.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Routing ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Identifier to map this listener with its corresponding call.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether this source should only be executed on the primary node when running in a cluster.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ServerStreamingAttributes">Server Streaming Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Client](#Client)

## \[Client\] Server Stream Listener

`<grpc:server-streaming-delegated-listener>`

Connects to a Server Streaming RPC. Server responses from the corresponding Server Streaming
Delegator are collected here. The Server Streaming Delegator and Server Streaming Delegated Listener
are mapped to each other via the routing ID.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Routing ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Identifier to map this listener with its corresponding delegator.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether this source should only be executed on the primary node when running in a cluster.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>
