- [Client](#Client)

## \[Client\] Server Stream Subscriber

`<grpc:server-streaming-subscriber-source>`

Connect to a Server Streaming RPC. This source acts as a subscriber by sending the request when it
starts. The request is provided at design time through the payload attribute.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Method</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The gRPC method name to invoke.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the gRPC request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Deadline</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Deadline">Deadline</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the deadline for this specific gRPC call. Overrides the configuration-level deadline if specified.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether this source should only be executed on the primary node when running in a cluster.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The path to the file containing the request payload.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Client](#Client)

## \[Server\] Bidirectional Streaming Listener

`<grpc:bidirectional-streaming-method>`

This source handles multiple client requests and produces multiple server responses.

In bidirectional streaming:

- The client sends multiple messages (independently).
- The server sends multiple responses (independently).
- Both streams operate independently and can send or receive messages at any time.

Flow Execution Model:

- The flow executes once per client message (automatic iteration; execution might be in parallel for
  different messages).
- A shared `streamState` map (`Map<String, Object>`) persists across all flow executions for the
  same stream.
- Each flow execution returns a response as the payload and can update state via
  `<grpc:response-bidi>`.
- When the client completes sending messages, the flow is executed one last time with
  `clientStreamCompleted=true`.

Example Use Cases:

- Interactive Processing (such as market ticker subscriptions)
  - Client sends subscription requests.
  - Server responds with price updates as they change.

- Client-controlled Streaming (such as paginated data export)
  - Client sends flow control tokens.
  - Server sends data chunks on demand.

Example usage in a Mule flow:

```xml
<flow name="MarketTicker">
  <grpc:bidirectional-streaming-method config-ref="GRPC_Config" methodName="market.Market/SubscribeTickers">
    <grpc:response-bidi streamStates="#[vars.updatedStreamStates]"/>
  </grpc:bidirectional-streaming-method>
  <!-- Calculate response and updated state -->
  <ee:transform>
    <ee:set-payload>
      <!-- Response protobuf message -->
    </ee:set-payload>
    <ee:variables>
      <ee:set-variable variableName="updatedStreamStates">
        <![CDATA[
        {
          subscriberCount: (attributes.streamState.subscriberCount default 0) + 1
        }
        ]]>
      </ee:set-variable>
    </ee:variables>
  </ee:transform>
</flow>
```

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Method Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The gRPC method name to invoke.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this source outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this source outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>On Capacity Overload</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>DROP</p></li><li><p>WAIT</p></li><li><p>FAIL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Strategy applied when the flow receives more messages than it has capacity to handle.</p></td><td><p>WAIT</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether this source should only be executed on the primary node when running in a cluster.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream Output</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#BidiStreamingOutput">Bidi Streaming Output</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Output parameter group for bidirectional streaming responses.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#BidiStreamingServerAttributes">Bidi Streaming Server Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [GRPC Server Configuration](#Server)

## \[Server\] Client Stream Listener

`<grpc:client-streaming-method>`

This source handles multiple client requests and produces a single response. In client streaming,
the client sends a stream of requests and the server responds with a single response after
processing all the requests.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Method Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The gRPC method name to invoke.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this source outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this source outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>On Capacity Overload</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>WAIT</p></li><li><p>DROP</p></li><li><p>FAIL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Strategy applied when the flow receives more messages than it has capacity to handle.</p></td><td><p>WAIT</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether this source should only be executed on the primary node when running in a cluster.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ClientStreamingOutput">Client Streaming Output</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Output structure for client streaming methods.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ClientStreamingServerAttributes">Client Streaming Server Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [GRPC Server Configuration](#Server)

## \[Server\] Server Stream Listener

`<grpc:server-streaming-method>`

This source handles a single client request and produces multiple responses.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Method Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The gRPC method name to invoke.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Concurrent Requests</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>If greater than 0, limits concurrent requests for this method (explicit backpressure).</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this source outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this source outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>On Capacity Overload</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>WAIT</p></li><li><p>DROP</p></li><li><p>FAIL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Strategy applied when the flow receives more messages than it has capacity to handle.</p></td><td><p>WAIT</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether this source should only be executed on the primary node when running in a cluster.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ServerStreamingServerAttributes">Server Streaming Server Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [GRPC Server Configuration](#Server)

## \[Server\] Unary Listener

`<grpc:unary-method>`

This source handles a single client request and produces a single response.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Method Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The gRPC method name to invoke.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this source outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this source outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether this source should only be executed on the primary node when running in a cluster.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Unary</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>The response content to send to the client.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [GRPC Server Configuration](#Server)

## Types

### Reconnection

Configures a reconnection strategy for an operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

Configures a standard reconnection strategy, which specifies how often to reconnect and how many
reconnection attempts the connector source or operation can make.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts to make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector source or operation attempts to
reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### TLS

Configures TLS to provide secure communications for the Mule app.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Trust Store</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configuration for the trust store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Key Store</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configuration for the key store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#StandardRevocationCheck">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CustomOcspResponder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CrlFile">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configuration for certificate revocation checking.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Trust Store

Configures the truststore for TLS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the trust store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the trust store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of store used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The algorithm used by the trust store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule stops performing certificate validations. Setting this to <code>true</code> can make connections vulnerable to attacks.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Key Store

Configures the keystore for the TLS protocol. The keystore you generate contains a private key and a
public certificate.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the key store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of store used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the key store contains many private keys, this attribute indicates the alias of the key that should be used. If not defined, the first key in the file will be used by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the key store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The algorithm used by the key store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

Configures standard revocation checks for TLS certificates.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Only verify the last element of the certificate chain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Try CRL instead of OCSP first.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Do not use the secondary checking method (the one not selected before).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Avoid verification failure when the revocation server can not be reached or is busy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom OCSP Responder

Configures a custom OCSP responder for certification revocation checks.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The URL of the OCSP responder.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cert Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response (must be in the trust store), if present.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### CRL File

Specifies the location of the certification revocation list (CRL) file.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path to the CRL file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Deadline

Configures the deadline for gRPC calls. A deadline specifies the maximum duration for a gRPC
operation to complete. If the operation exceeds this deadline, it is automatically cancelled.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Duration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The duration value for the deadline.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>10</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Units</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The time unit for the <strong>Duration</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

Configures the maximum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before
Mule considers it eligible for expiration.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance should be allowed to be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit for the <strong>Max Idle Time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Server Streaming Attributes

Represents attributes or metadata for streaming operations. This class provides channel and routing
information for gRPC streaming calls.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>End Of Stream</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether the stream has ended.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether an error occurred during streaming.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Message</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The error message if an error occurred.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Request Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The request identifier.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Routing Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The routing identifier.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

Configures the in-memory streaming strategy by which the request fails if the data exceeds the MAX
buffer size. Always run performance tests to find the optimal buffer size for your specific use
case.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The amount of memory that will be allocated to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can be fit into this buffer, then the buffer expands according to the bufferSizeIncrement attribute, with an upper limit of maxInMemorySize.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised when the buffer gets full.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum amount of memory to use. If more than that is used then a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised. A value lower than or equal to zero means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which all these attributes are expressed.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

Configures the repeatable file-store streaming strategy by which Mule keeps a portion of the stream
content in memory. If the stream content is larger than the configured buffer size, Mule backs up
the bufferâs content to disk and then clears the memory.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the maximum memory that the stream should use to keep data in memory. If more than that is consumed content on the disk is buffered.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which <strong>In Memory Size</strong> is expressed.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

Configures the redelivery policy for executing requests that generate errors. You can add a
redelivery policy to any source in a flow.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Redelivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum number of times a message can be redelivered and processed unsuccessfully before triggering process-failed-message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Digest Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The secure hashing algorithm to use. If not set, the default is SHA-256.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Identifier</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicyMessageIdentifier">Redelivery Policy Message Identifier</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines which strategy is used to identify the messages.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ObjectStore</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The object store where the redelivery counter for each message is stored.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy Message Identifier

Configures how to identify a redelivered message and how to find out when the message was
redelivered.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Use Secure Hash</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines one or more expressions to use to determine when a message has been redelivered. This property may only be set if useSecureHash is false.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Error Mapping

Configures the error mapping.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Source</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ANY</p></li><li><p>REDELIVERY_EXHAUSTED</p></li><li><p>TRANSFORMATION</p></li><li><p>EXPRESSION</p></li><li><p>SECURITY</p></li><li><p>CLIENT_SECURITY</p></li><li><p>SERVER_SECURITY</p></li><li><p>ROUTING</p></li><li><p>CONNECTIVITY</p></li><li><p>RETRY_EXHAUSTED</p></li><li><p>TIMEOUT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The source error type to map.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The target error type to map to.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Client Streaming Attributes

Represents attributes or metadata for client streaming operations. Returned with every client
streaming operation response to indicate the current state of the conversation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>End Of Stream</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether the stream has ended.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Sent</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether a message was sent in this step.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Request Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The request identifier.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Bidi Streaming Server Attributes

Attributes for bidirectional streaming gRPC methods.

Bidirectional streaming allows both client and server to send multiple messages independently. The
client can send multiple requests while the server sends multiple responses.

Key characteristics:

- Flow executes once per client message (automatic iteration, parallel execution)
- Flow can send 0, 1, or N responses per message using
  [\[Server\] Respond Server Stream Message](#SendStreamMessage).
- Shared streamState map persists across all flow executions for state management
- Final execution with `clientStreamCompleted=true` when client finishes sending

Use cases:

- Interactive processing (such as market ticker subscriptions)
  - Client sends subscription requests.
  - Server sends price updates as they change.

- Client-controlled streaming (such as paginated data export)
  - Client sends flow control tokens.
  - Server sends data chunks on demand.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Client Stream Completed</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether the client has finished sending (true on final execution).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unique stream identifier.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Total messages received so far.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Index</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Current message number (1-based).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Method Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Fully qualified gRPC method name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Stream State</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Shared state map across all messages (thread-safe ConcurrentHashMap).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Timestamp</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the stream is initiated.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Bidi Streaming Output

Output parameter group for bidirectional streaming responses.

This class captures both the response (body) and the updated streaming state from flow execution.
The handler uses this to:

1.  Send the response as the gRPC reply.
2.  Update its local streamState with the returned values for the next message.

Example usage in a Mule flow:

```xml
<grpc:bidirectional-streaming-method methodName="retail.Retail/AddToCart">
  <grpc:stream-output streamState="#[vars.updatedStreamState]"/>
</grpc:bidirectional-streaming-method>

<ee:transform>
  <ee:set-payload>
    <!-- Response protobuf message -->
  </ee:set-payload>
  <ee:variables>
    <ee:set-variable variableName="updatedStreamState">
      {
        count: (attributes.streamState.count default 0) + payload.qty,
        amount: (attributes.streamState.amount default 0.0) + (payload.qty * payload.price)
      }
    </ee:set-variable>
  </ee:variables>
</ee:transform>
```

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Response</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The response message to send to the client.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Stream State</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Updated streaming state to persist for the next message. Values set here are available in attributes.streamState for subsequent messages.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[{}]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Client Streaming Server Attributes

Attributes for client streaming gRPC methods. Simplified to match apikit pattern: per-message
execution with stream state.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Message Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of messages received so far.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Stream Completed</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether this is the final message in the stream. When true, the flow should return the final response.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Stream State</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Shared mutable state across all messages. The flow can read or update this map to maintain aggregated values.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Client Streaming Output

Output structure for client streaming methods, matching apikit pattern.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Response</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The response message to send to the client (only used on final execution when client stream completes).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Stream State a</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Updated stream state to persist across messages. Values set here are available as <code>attributes.streamState</code> in subsequent executions.</p></div><div><p>For <code>Map&lt;String, Object&gt;</code> types, Mule SDK automatically generates corresponding XML:</p></div><div><p><code>&lt;grpc:stream-states&gt;&lt;grpc:stream-state key="â¦â" value="â¦â"/&gt;&lt;/grpc:stream-states&gt;</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[{}]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Server Streaming Server Attributes

Server-side attributes for server streaming gRPC methods. In server streaming, the client sends a
single request, the Mule flow executes, and the server sends multiple responses using
[\[Server\] Respond Server Stream Message](#SendStreamMessage) and
[\[Server\] End Stream](#EndStream).

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unique identifier for this streaming connection (used to route <a href="#SendStreamMessage">[Server] Respond Server Stream Message</a> and <a href="#EndStream">[Server] End Stream</a>).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Method Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Fully qualified gRPC method name (for example, <code>retail.Retail/PriceItemStream</code>).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Single Binary Protobuf

Protobuf contract parameterization. Provides unified access to protobuf descriptors and metadata
types for both client operations and server sources through the IdlDefinition interface.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Protobuf Descriptor File</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path to the Protocol Buffer descriptor file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Private Key Text

Private key provided as inline text.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The private key content.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Private Key File

Private key provided from a file.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Private Key File Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path to the file containing the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
---

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure gRPC Connector 1.0

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure a source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-source).
4.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
5.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).
6.  [Configure the other connector fields](#configure-other-fields).

When you run the connector, you can view the app log to check for problems in real time, as
described in [View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[gRPC Connector Reference](grpc-connector-reference).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure Anypoint Connector for gRPC (gRPC
Connector):

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add gRPC Connector to your Mule project to automatically populate the XML code with the connectorâs
namespace and schema location and add the required dependencies to the projectâs `pom.xml` file:

1.  In **Mule Palette**, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In **Add Dependencies to Project**, type `grpc` in the search field.
3.  Click **gRPC Connector** in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure a Source

A source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. You can configure one of these sources
to use with gRPC Connector:

- **HTTP > Listener**

  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port

- **Scheduler**

  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

- **gRPC Connector sources**

  See [gRPC Connector 1.0 Reference](grpc-connector-reference) for sources.

For example, to configure an **HTTP > Listener** source, follow these steps:

1.  In **Mule Palette**, select **HTTP > Listener**.
2.  Drag **Listener** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **Listener** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the **Display Name**
    field.
4.  Specify a value for the **Path** field.
5.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of the **HTTP > Listener** source in the app.
6.  On the **General** tab, specify the connection information for the connector.
7.  On the **TLS** tab, optionally specify the TLS information for the connector.
8.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
9.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
10. Click **OK**.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you are specifying an action for that connector to
perform.

To add an operation for gRPC Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In **Mule Palette**, select **gRPC** and then select the desired operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas, next to the source.

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure the connector, configure a global element that all instances of that connector in
the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the connection settings the
connector needs to access the gRPC service or expose a gRPC server.

To configure the global element for gRPC Connector, follow these steps:

1.  Select the operation or source in the Studio canvas.
2.  In the **General** configuration screen for the operation or source, click the **Add** icon to
    access the global element configuration fields.
3.  In the **General** tab, in **Connection**, select the configuration type to use:
    - **Client** for connecting to gRPC services as a client. Then select the connection type:
      - [Google Service Account Auth Provider](grpc-connector-reference#Client_GoogleServiceAccountAuthProvider)
      - [No Auth](grpc-connector-reference#Client_NoAuth)
      - [Oauth Client Credentials](grpc-connector-reference#Client_OauthClientCredentials)

    - **Server** for exposing gRPC methods. Then configure
      [Grpc Server](grpc-connector-reference#Server_GrpcServer).

      You can reference a configuration file that contains ANT-style property placeholders
      (recommended), or you can enter your connection details in the global configuration
      properties. For information about the benefits of using property placeholders and how to
      configure them, refer to
      [Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview).

4.  In the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
5.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server (for client
    configuration).
6.  Click **OK**.

## Configure Additional Connector Fields

After you configure a global element for gRPC Connector, configure the other required fields for the
connector. The required fields vary depending on which connector operation you use.

## View the App Log

To check for problems, you can view the app log as follows:

- If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the app log output is visible on the Anypoint
  Studio console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log output is visible in your
  operating system console.

Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view the
app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`. You can configure the location of
the log path in the app log file `log4j2.xml`.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Using Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio)
- [gRPC Connector Reference](grpc-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# gRPC Connector 1.0 XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Although you can manually code a Mule app in XML, it is more efficient to use Anypoint Code Builder
or Studio:

- If you manually code a Mule runtime engine (Mule) app in XML, either from the Anypoint Code
  Builder or Studio XML editor, or from a text editor, you can access the connector from your app by
  adding reference statements to both your XML Mule flow and the Apache Maven `pom.xml` file.
- If you add the connector in Anypoint Code Builder or Studio, the XML is automatically populated
  the code with the connectorâs namespace and schema location and it also adds a dependency to the
  `pom.xml` file.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `mule` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/grpc
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/grpc/current/mule-grpc.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the `<mule>` XML block:

```xml
<mule xmlns:grpc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/grpc"
	xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/grpc
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/grpc/current/mule-grpc.xsd">
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Apache Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app.
If you code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access to
this connector:

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule-grpc-connector</artifactId>
  <version>x.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `x.x.x` with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information:

1.  Go to [Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/).
2.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
3.  In Exchange, search for `grpc`.
4.  Select the connector.
5.  Click **Dependency Snippets** near the upper right of the screen.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# gRPC Connector 1.0

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for gRPC (gRPC Connector) enables you to connect to the gRPC API. gRPC Connector
enables Mule applications to communicate with gRPC services, providing both client and server
capabilities for high-performance, cross-platform RPC communication.

For information about compatibility and fixed issues, refer to the gRPC Connector Release Notes.

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The gRPC Connectorâs API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements and global elements in a Mule flow
- How to create a Mule app using Anypoint Code Builder or Anypoint Studio

Before creating an app, you must have:

- Credentials to access the gRPC target resource
- Anypoint Platform
- The latest versions of Anypoint Code Builder or Anypoint Studio

## Supported gRPC Patterns

This connector supports all four gRPC communication patterns.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Pattern</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Unary</p></td><td><p>Single request, single response</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Server Streaming</p></td><td><p>Single request, multiple responses streamed from server</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Streaming</p></td><td><p>Multiple requests from client, single response</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Bidirectional Streaming</p></td><td><p>Multiple requests and responses streamed in both directions</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Key Capabilities

- Protocol Buffer Support

  Native support for protobuf descriptor files (`.desc`)

- Dynamic Metadata Resolution

  Automatic type resolution based on IDL definitions

- Connection Management

  Managed channels with connection pooling and validation

- Conversation Management

  Support for stateful streaming conversations

- Async Operations

  Non-blocking asynchronous operation execution

- TLS Support

  Configurable TLS/SSL for secure connections

- Custom Headers

  Support for default headers on connections

## Architecture

### Component Diagram

![A flowchart illustrating the architecture of a gRPC connector within a Mule application as it communicates with a gRPC server](_images/grpc-connector-architecture.png)

### Conversation Management

The connector maintains stateful conversations for streaming operations:

- Server Conversations

  Maps `conversationId` to `ServerConversation` containing shared FrameWrapperQueue for multiple
  streaming requests

- Client Conversations

  Maps conversationId to ClientConversation with request observer and response future

- Bidirectional Conversations

  Maps `conversationId` to `BidiConversation` for bidirectional streaming with request or response
  observers

## Next Step

After you complete the prerequisites, youâre ready to create an app and configure the connector
using [Anypoint Studio](grpc-connector-studio) or [Anypoint Code Builder](grpc-connector-acb).
